description: Daily driver usability improvements: diff mode persistence, sidebar rail fix, resizable panels everywhere, file tree view, full file viewing.

userStories.title: Diff View Mode Persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want my diff view preference (inline vs split) to persist across files and restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add diffViewMode: 'inline' | 'side-by-side' to LayoutState in useLayoutState.ts with default 'inline'
DiffViewer accepts an onModeChange callback prop that fires when user toggles mode
AppLayout passes layout.diffViewMode as initialMode to DiffViewer (threaded through RepoView)
AppLayout passes a setDiffViewMode callback that updates layout state when mode changes
Opening a new file uses the persisted mode, not resetting to inline
Mode survives app restart via existing localStorage persistence
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DiffViewer.tsx has local useState for mode. Need to lift it: add onModeChange callback prop, call it when user clicks inline/split toggle. AppLayout reads from useLayoutState and passes down. The DiffViewer is rendered in RepoView.tsx when viewingDiff is true.

userStories.title: Fix Sidebar Icon Rail Overlays
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar icon rail buttons to open floating overlays with section content.
acceptanceCriteria: The overlay code exists in Sidebar.tsx (handleRailIconClick, railOverlay, RAIL_SECTIONS) — verify it renders visually
If overlays don't appear, the likely cause is overflow: hidden on the sidebar container div in AppLayout.tsx — change to overflow: visible or move the overlay to a portal
The overlay appears to the right of the icon rail with proper z-index (above center panel content)
Overlay shows full section content: branches list, remotes, tags, stashes, submodules, files
Clicking outside the overlay or pressing Escape closes it
Clicking a different rail icon switches the overlay content
The expand button (top PanelLeftOpen icon) still expands the full sidebar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The overlay implementation ALREADY EXISTS in Sidebar.tsx (lines 1988-2120). The bug is likely that the parent div in AppLayout.tsx has overflow: hidden which clips the absolute-positioned overlay. The sidebar container div at AppLayout line ~373 has style={{ overflow: 'hidden' }} — this needs to change to overflow: visible when collapsed, or the overlay needs to use a React portal to render outside the container. Check z-index too — the overlay needs z-index higher than the center panel.

userStories.title: Resizable Right Panel Width
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag the right panel wider or narrower.
acceptanceCriteria: A 5px vertical drag handle div between the center panel and the right panel in AppLayout
Drag handle shows cursor: col-resize on hover with subtle var(--border) background highlight
Dragging resizes the right panel width in real time (min 280px, max 600px, default 340px)
Double-click resets to 340px default
Activate rightPanelSize in useLayoutState — change default from percentage (25) to pixels (340), add clamping 280-600 on load
Right panel width reads from layout.rightPanelSize instead of hardcoded 340
Width persists across restarts via existing debounced save
Reuse the same drag handle pattern from the sidebar (isDraggingRef, mousedown/mousemove/mouseup on document, body class for user-select:none)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Copy the sidebar drag handle pattern from AppLayout.tsx lines 230-266. The right panel drag handle goes BETWEEN the center div and the right panel div. Drag direction: dragging LEFT makes the right panel wider. The rightPanelSize field already exists in LayoutState but stores a percentage — change it to pixels and add migration (if < 100, multiply by 12.8 and clamp).

userStories.title: Resizable Detail vs Staging Vertical Split
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag the boundary between commit details and staging area in the right panel.
acceptanceCriteria: A 5px horizontal drag handle div between the detail panel section and the staging area section
Drag handle shows cursor: row-resize on hover with subtle var(--border) background highlight
Dragging resizes the vertical split ratio in real time
Minimum height for either section: 100px
Default split: 60% detail / 40% staging
Add detailStagingSplit: number (0-100, percent for detail share) to LayoutState with default 60
The split ratio replaces the current hardcoded flex: 6 / flex: 4 — use calculated pixel heights based on container height and the ratio
Split persists across restarts
Drag handle uses clientY delta instead of clientX (vertical dragging)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Similar to sidebar/right panel drag handle but vertical. Track container height via ref, calculate detail height as containerHeight * (detailStagingSplit / 100). The drag delta adjusts the percentage. Clamp so neither section goes below 100px. Use the same isDragging ref pattern.

userStories.title: Changed Files Tree View
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle between flat path list and directory tree view for changed files in commit details.
acceptanceCriteria: Two small toggle buttons in the files section header: List icon (path view) and FolderTree icon (tree view)
Path view: current flat list behavior — unchanged
Tree view: files grouped into collapsible directory tree — directories are expandable nodes, files are leaves
Tree nodes show: FolderOpen/Folder icon for directories, status icon (FilePlus/FileEdit/FileMinus) for files
Files in tree view show +/- insertion/deletion counts
Clicking a file in tree view triggers onFileClick callback (same as path view)
Selected file highlighted in both views (selectedFilePath prop)
Add fileListView: 'path' | 'tree' to LayoutState with default 'path'
View mode persists across restarts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Build a utility function buildFileTree(files: CommitFileDetail[]) that returns a tree: { name: string, fullPath: string, isDir: boolean, children: TreeNode[], file?: CommitFileDetail }. Split each file path by '/' and insert into the tree. Render with a recursive FileTreeNode component. Directories start expanded by default.

userStories.title: View Full File Content at Commit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the full content of a file at a specific commit, not just the diff.
acceptanceCriteria: A toggle in the center diff header bar: 'Diff' (default) and 'File' buttons
Diff view: current behavior — green/red additions/deletions
File view: fetches full file content at that commit via git show HASH:PATH
File view has syntax highlighting (reuse highlightLine from DiffViewer or CodeEditor)
File view shows line numbers
Add IPC handler showFileAtCommit(repoPath, hash, filePath) in git-service.ts that runs git show HASH:PATH
Register handler in git-ipc.ts, add to preload API, add TypeScript type declaration
Binary files show 'Binary file — cannot display' message
Toggle does NOT persist — always defaults to Diff (diff is the primary use case)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Need changes in 4 layers: (1) git-service.ts — add showFileAtCommit method using git show, (2) git-ipc.ts — register IPC handler, (3) preload/index.ts — expose to renderer, (4) useLayoutState.ts type declaration — add to electronAPI type. In RepoView.tsx, add state for viewMode ('diff'|'file'), fetch file content when 'file' is selected, render with a simple pre+code block with syntax highlighting.
